topStories.headline: Park Tavern Closing for Good on June 21, Ending a Tumultuous Run on Washington Square
topStories.dek: The acclaimed North Beach brasserie, once a James Beard semifinalist, will shut its doors permanently on Father's Day after a revival that lasted barely 19 months.
topStories.body:
Park Tavern, the North Beach restaurant at 1652 Stockton Street overlooking Washington Square Park, announced Wednesday on Instagram that it will close permanently on June 21. The closure ends a brief revival that began in November 2024 when original partner James Nicholas and celebrity chef Jonathan Waxman attempted to breathe new life into the space.

Park Tavern first opened in 2011 and was named a semifinalist for the James Beard Foundation's Best New Restaurant award within its first year. It became a neighborhood institution before a series of ownership disputes, the pandemic, and financial challenges forced it to close. Nicholas relaunched the restaurant with Waxman — a Chez Panisse alum and James Beard 'Best Chef in New York City' winner — and original chef Jennifer Puccio, but the comeback lasted roughly 19 months.

The restaurant's Instagram post thanked staff and guests, saying it had been their privilege to serve the community. In a bittersweet coincidence, June 21 is Father's Day, and the restaurant's website still lists a special Father's Day brunch from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. — meaning the final service may well be pouring mimosas for North Beach dads.

The address at 1652 Stockton has a storied restaurant history. Before Park Tavern, the space was home to Moose's from 1992 to 2005, which succeeded the legendary Washington Square Bar & Grill. Whatever comes next inherits a corner with three decades of San Francisco dining legacy.

topStories.headline: 72nd Annual North Beach Festival Returns June 20–21 with 200+ Vendors on 11 Blocks
topStories.dek: San Francisco's oldest street fair takes over Grant Avenue and Columbus Avenue on Father's Day weekend with live music, beer gardens, Italian street painting, and the annual Blessing of the Animals.
topStories.body:
The 72nd annual North Beach Festival, one of the country's original outdoor festivals, will fill the heart of the neighborhood on Saturday and Sunday, June 20–21, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. The free festival spans 11 blocks along Grant Avenue and Columbus Avenue around Washington Square Park, with more than 200 vendors offering art, crafts, jewelry, vintage fashion, and home décor.

New this year, organizers have added two blocks of Stockton Street to the festival footprint. Four beer gardens will serve a variety of libations alongside live music on three stages. The daily Blessing of the Animals ceremony takes place at 1 p.m. at the National Shrine of Saint Francis of Assisi, 610 Vallejo Street.

The Makers Block will offer interactive art activities led by local artists, including live painting, silk-screening, and jewelry making — participants can create their own commemorative medallion from recycled aluminum. Presented by Zoox, the festival is hosted by the North Beach Business Association, with all proceeds granted to local community organizations supporting free meals, after-school activities, job training, and temporary housing.

Festivalgoers can also take advantage of a free walking tour of North Beach departing from Saints Peter and Paul Church at 10 a.m. on Saturday morning, offered by the San Francisco Public Library.

topStories.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Plaza Nears Completion as $10 Million Makeover Targets Summer Opening
topStories.dek: The new public plaza on the site of the demolished Alioto's restaurant is on track to open this summer, opening up Golden Gate Bridge views and Inner Lagoon access for the first time in decades.
topStories.body:
The Fisherman's Wharf Forward project — a $10 million initiative by the Port of San Francisco to reinvigorate the neighborhood — is approaching its summer 2026 target for completing the new public plaza where the historic Alioto's restaurant once stood. Demolition of the nearly century-old building began in early January, and plaza construction has been underway since February.

Designed by San Francisco-based SITELAB urban studio, the roughly 5,000-square-foot plaza on Taylor Street will feature wave-like seating, benches overlooking the Inner Lagoon, interpretive signage about the wharf's fishing heritage, upgraded lighting, and space for pop-up retail and beverage service. The ground treatment incorporates a 2:1 scale blueprint of a historic Monterey fishing boat. The plaza will open up views of the Golden Gate Bridge, San Francisco Bay, and docked fishing boats that were previously blocked by the shuttered building.

Additional near-term improvements include a new Inner Lagoon overlook along Al Scoma Way and new lighting to showcase the fishing fleet. Some community members have pushed to name the plaza after the Alioto family, who first came to San Francisco over 100 years ago. The Port is also entering a longer-term phase of community engagement on seismic and sea-level-rise resilience improvements along Taylor Street.

Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District Executive Director Bri Maughan has called the investment 'the kind of investment our neighborhood has long deserved,' noting the project will ensure the wharf is thriving for generations to come.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Watch Zone at Pier 39
events.description: Pier 39 is one of three anchor watch zones in San Francisco for the FIFA World Cup 2026, with large outdoor screens showing matches throughout the tournament. Free and open to the public.

events.title: Dear San Francisco: The Pride Edition at Club Fugazi
events.location: Club Fugazi, 678 Green Street, North Beach
events.description: The critically acclaimed immersive circus show celebrates Pride Month with rotating special guests including Rotimi Agbabiaka (June 11–14), Sara Felder (June 19–21), and circus artists Joey Moore and Zed Cézard (June 24–27). Tickets from $59.

events.title: 72nd Annual North Beach Festival
events.location: Grant Avenue and Columbus Avenue, North Beach
events.description: San Francisco's oldest street fair returns with 200+ vendors on 11 blocks, four beer gardens, live music on three stages, Italian street painting, Blessing of the Animals at 1 p.m., and a kids' Fun Zone. Free admission.

events.title: Juneteenth Parade and Festival at the Embarcadero
events.location: Market Street at 2nd Street to Embarcadero Plaza
events.description: San Francisco's fourth annual Juneteenth Parade travels along Market Street to Embarcadero Plaza, followed by a festival with live music, a classic car showcase, dance performances, and cultural tributes. Free.

events.title: SF Pride Parade
events.location: Market Street from Embarcadero to Civic Center
events.description: The 56th annual San Francisco Pride Parade under the theme 'Resistance in Action!' begins at Embarcadero Plaza and proceeds up Market Street. Over 250 contingents, free and open to all. The Pride Celebration takes place June 27–28 at Civic Center.

events.title: Park Tavern Final Service — Father's Day Brunch
events.location: Park Tavern, 1652 Stockton Street, North Beach
events.description: The acclaimed North Beach brasserie hosts its final service with a Father's Day brunch, closing permanently after nearly 15 years at its Washington Square address. Reservations recommended.

events.title: Free Walking Tour of North Beach
events.location: Saints Peter and Paul Church, 666 Filbert Street, North Beach
events.description: Free walking tour of the historic North Beach neighborhood offered by the San Francisco Public Library, departing before the North Beach Festival opens.

moreNews.headline: Club Fugazi Partners with Hilda and Jesse and Humphry Slocombe for Pride Dining Experiences
moreNews.body: Throughout June, Club Fugazi is offering a Pride-themed dinner-and-show package pairing a four-course meal at nearby Hilda and Jesse with a performance of Dear San Francisco. Packages are $200 per person. Additionally, queer-owned Humphry Slocombe has teamed up with Hilda and Jesse on a limited-edition 'Pancakes Without Boundaries' ice cream flavor, available at all Bay Area scoop shops and at Club Fugazi throughout June.

moreNews.headline: Pier 39 Among Three Anchor Fan Zones for FIFA World Cup 2026
moreNews.body: Pier 39 at Fisherman's Wharf has been designated one of three anchor fan zones in San Francisco for the FIFA World Cup 2026, running from June 12 through July 19. The other two citywide zones are at Thrive City (Chase Center) and China Basin Park at Mission Rock. All feature large outdoor screens and are free and open to the public. The Bay Area Host Committee is coordinating more than 30 official watch events across the region during the tournament.

moreNews.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Promenade Gets New Maritime Ground Mural
moreNews.body: A new ground mural celebrating Fisherman's Wharf's maritime heritage has been installed along the Promenade walkway between Pier 43 and Pier 45. The colorful installation, created through a community painting event in early June in partnership with Street Plans, features clusters of Bay-native fish species important to the wharf's commercial and sportfishing industry, including lingcod, King salmon, and leopard sharks.
